title: [IR] Fix canReplacePointersIfEqual to properly validate vector pointers
author: ParkHanbum
description:
Previously, `canReplacePointersIfEqual` unconditionally returned
`true` for vectors of pointers (e.g., `<2 x ptr>`) because it only
checked for scalar pointer types.

This resulted in a failure to perform appropriate verification for
these types. This patch fixes the logic to ensure they are properly
validated.

Fixes https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues/174045

tests.test_body:
define void @select_same_obj(i1 %c, ptr %p, i64 %x) {
entry:
  %p2 = getelementptr i8, ptr %p, i64 %x
  %p3 = select i1 %c, ptr %p, ptr %p2
  %cmp = icmp eq ptr %p, %p3
  br i1 %cmp, label %if, label %exit

if:                                               ; preds = %entry
  call void @use_ptr(ptr %p)
  call void @use_ptr(ptr %p3)
  ret void

exit:                                             ; preds = %entry
  ret void
}

declare void @use_ptr(ptr)

define ptr @ptr_eq_replace_same_underlying_object(ptr %st, i64 %i, i64 %j) {
  %a = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %st, i64 %i
  %b = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %st, i64 %j
  %cmp = icmp eq ptr %a, %b
  %sel = select i1 %cmp, ptr %a, ptr %b
  ret ptr %sel
}

comments.author: ParkHanbum
comments.body:
@nikic 

```
define <2 x ptr> @test_vector_ptr_const_val(<2 x ptr> %a) {
  %cmp = icmp eq <2 x ptr> %a, <ptr inttoptr (i64 42 to ptr), ptr inttoptr (i64 88 to ptr)>
  %sel = select <2 x i1> %cmp, <2 x ptr> %a, <2 x ptr> <ptr inttoptr (i64 42 to ptr), ptr inttoptr (i64 88 to ptr)>
  ret <2 x ptr> %sel
}
```
I tested it this way—is this what you intended? 
A crash occurs, and the log is as follows. 

```
opt: /home/m/llvm/llvm/lib/Analysis/Loads.cpp:76: bool isDereferenceableAndAlignedPointer(const llvm::Value*, llvm::Align, const llvm::APInt&, const llvm::DataLayout&, const llvm::Instruction*, llvm::AssumptionCache*, const llvm::DominatorTree*, const llvm::TargetLibraryInfo*, llvm::SmallPtrSetImpl<const llvm::Value*>&, unsigned int): Assertion `V->getType()->isPointerTy() && "Base must be pointer"' failed.
#11 0x000055faf6a683a4 isDereferenceableAndAlignedPointer(llvm::Value const*, llvm::Align, llvm::APInt const&, llvm::DataLayout const&, llvm::Instruction const*, llvm::AssumptionCache*, llvm::DominatorTree const*, llvm::TargetLibraryInfo const*, llvm::SmallPtrSetImpl<llvm::Value const*>&, unsigned int) Loads.cpp:0:0
#12 0x000055faf6a6927b llvm::isDereferenceableAndAlignedPointer(llvm::Value const*, llvm::Type*, llvm::Align, llvm::DataLayout const&, llvm::Instruction const*, llvm::AssumptionCache*, llvm::DominatorTree const*, llvm::TargetLibraryInfo const*) (bin/opt+0x4a8127b)

```
I am currently reviewing it.
